title: Competitor EDA: UX Polish & Data Accuracy Fixes
purpose: Close three UX gaps left open after the v1 refactor: (1) eliminate the ~500ms latency on author row click by filtering the heatmap client-side, (2) prevent avgViewsPerPost inflation from notice posts even when excludeNotices=false, (3) add a visible empty-state when a selected author has zero posts in the current range.
design_intent: No layout changes. Incremental correctness improvements: faster interaction feedback, more accurate derived metric, safer zero-data state. Change impact is localized to lib/competitorAnalytics.ts and src/AnalyticsPage.tsx.
change_impact: Modifies src/AnalyticsPage.tsx (client-side heatmap filter, empty-state) and lib/competitorAnalytics.ts (avgViewsPerPost notice exclusion). No changes to server.ts, scheduler, bot.ts, or publisher flow.

gaps_being_closed.title: focusAuthor refetch latency
gaps_being_closed.observed: Clicking a row triggers a full API refetch. On a cold server this adds ~500ms of perceived latency between the click and the heatmap update.
gaps_being_closed.root_cause: selectedAuthor is included in queryUrl, causing useEffect to re-fire the full fetch. The heatmap is the only visual that needs per-author data; timeSeries and summary are always aggregate.
gaps_being_closed.fix: Split the data model into a persistent base fetch (no focusAuthor) and a derived client-side heatmap filter. The base fetch re-runs only when date/bucket/excludeNotices change. Row click filters heatmap cells in memory — no network round-trip.
gaps_being_closed.failure_prevention_reason: Eliminates a noticeable interaction lag that undermines the drill-down UX. Also removes unnecessary server load on every row click.

gaps_being_closed.title: avgViewsPerPost inflated by notice posts
gaps_being_closed.observed: When excludeNotices=false, notice posts (isNotice=true) are included in postsInRange but their views (often 0 or near-0) dilute the avgViewsPerPost calculation for authors who mix regular and notice posts.
gaps_being_closed.root_cause: avgViewsPerPost = Math.round(totalViews / postsInRange) uses all posts including notices.
gaps_being_closed.fix: Compute avgViewsPerPost using only non-notice events regardless of the excludeNotices query param. Add avgViewsPostCount (number of non-notice posts used for the denominator) to AuthorSummaryRow so the UI can show a footnote when it differs from postsInRange.
gaps_being_closed.failure_prevention_reason: Prevents misleading quality signal. An author with 10 regular posts (avg 50 views) and 5 notice posts (0 views) should show 50, not 33.

gaps_being_closed.title: No empty-state for single-author mode with zero posts
gaps_being_closed.observed: If a user selects an author and then changes the date range to a window where that author posted nothing, the timeline renders empty axes with no explanation.
gaps_being_closed.root_cause: No guard on selectedAuthor presence in timeSeries data. The existing empty check (timeSeries.length === 0) only covers the case where all authors have zero posts.
gaps_being_closed.fix: After author selection, check if any timeSeries bucket has a non-zero value for selectedAuthor. If all are zero, render an explanatory inline message instead of an empty chart: 'No posts from [author] in this range — select a different window or clear selection.'
gaps_being_closed.failure_prevention_reason: Prevents confusion where the user thinks the chart is broken or the data failed to load.

additions.description: Number of non-notice posts used as the denominator for avgViewsPerPost. Equal to postsInRange when excludeNotices=true; may differ when excludeNotices=false.
additions.why: Allows the UI to signal when avgViewsPerPost is computed on a subset of posts.

changed.before: Math.round(totalViews / postsInRange) — includes notice posts in denominator
changed.after: Math.round(nonNoticeViews / nonNoticePosts) — denominator is always non-notice posts only
changed.why: Fixes inflation bug regardless of query excludeNotices param.

focusAuthor_removal.description: focusAuthor query param is no longer sent by the frontend. The server still supports it for backward compatibility (no removal). Client derives the filtered heatmap from the base payload.

heatmap_filter.before: queryUrl includes focusAuthor → full API refetch on row click
heatmap_filter.after: Base fetch has no focusAuthor. On author select, heatmap cells are filtered in the browser from the full payload's heatmap data.
heatmap_filter.note: The base payload still returns all-author aggregate heatmap. Client-side filter reproduces focusAuthor behavior using events already in the payload. Because the payload does not carry per-author raw events, the filter must be reconstructed from heatmap cells that have an author field — OR the server adds a per-author cell map to the payload.
heatmap_filter.implementation_choice: Extend HeatmapCell with optional author field. Server populates author on each cell. Client filters cells by selectedAuthor on render. No new API endpoint needed.

empty_state.trigger: selectedAuthor is set AND all timeSeries rows have 0 for selectedAuthor dataKey
empty_state.render: Inline notice replacing the LineChart: 'No posts from {selectedAuthor} in this range. Change the date range or clear the selection.'
empty_state.style: Same amber-tinted notice style used elsewhere in the page.

avgViewsPerPost_footnote.render: Superscript asterisk on the Avg Views/Post cell; footnote below table: '* Avg views computed on non-notice posts only.'
avgViewsPerPost_footnote.when_visible: Only when excludeNotices=false and the author has a mix of notice and regular posts.

acceptance_criteria: Clicking an author row does NOT trigger a network request (verify in browser devtools: no XHR/fetch after initial load when only selectedAuthor changes)
Heatmap updates to reflect selected author immediately on row click without a loading flash
avgViewsPerPost for an author with 5 notice posts (0 views each) and 10 regular posts (50 avg views each) = 50, not 33
avgViewsPostCount field present on every AuthorSummaryRow; equals postsInRange when author has no notice posts
When selectedAuthor is set and has zero posts in the current range, the timeline is replaced by an inline explanatory message
When selectedAuthor has posts in range, behavior is identical to v1 (single orange line)
No changes to scheduler, bot.ts, publisher flow, or any non-analytics API endpoints
out_of_scope: Any change to the server-side focusAuthor query param handling
